It shifts the filtering cost from the receiver to the sender. Salmention, to propagate threads: if someone replies to a comment on my post, the original post finds out by re-sending update webmentions to everyone involved. You don't escape spam or moderation. However, it is a good foundation for building a distributed comment system. What if your website has no backend? You are not left out: webmention.io receives webmentions on your behalf. You add a in your HTML pointing to the service and it gives you an API to query and display them. It is exactly what you need if you use a static site generator like Hugo, Jekyll or Eleventy. IndieAuth: your domain as your login IndieAuth answers the question: what if your identity for signing in were your URL, instead of "you on Google" or "you on Facebook"? Technically it is OAuth 2.0, the standard for authentication and authorization. Both users and applications are identified by URLs, which removes the need for prior client registration ("IndieAuth uses DNS as a replacement for client registration"), and PKCE is mandatory (a security mechanism that prevents an attacker from stealing the access token). The flow in a nutshell: you type your domain in the login form, the service fetches your page and discovers your authorization server via rel="indieauth-metadata", redirects you there, you authenticate however you prefer (password, email, RelMeAuth), and the service receives confirmation that you control that URL. It is a living standard that the community considers stable. Micropub: publish from any client Micropub, a W3C Recommendation since May 2017, separates the publishing interface from your site's software: any application (web, iOS, Android) can create, edit and delete posts on your domain. It replaces the old MetaWeblog and AtomPub, which depended on sharing your password, with OAuth tokens obtained via IndieAuth. The beautiful part is its vocabulary: it does not invent one, it is serialized microformats. Creating a post is a POST with h=entry and the same h-entry properties: curl https://your-site.com/micropub \ -d h=entry \ -d "content=Hello world" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer XXXXXXX" If you are already used to working with REST APIs, it will feel very natural. WebSub: real-time feeds WebSub, formerly known as PubSubHubbub, a W3C Recommendation since January 2018, removes feed polling: instead of a thousand readers asking your server every half hour whether there is something new, you notify a hub when you publish and the hub instantly notifies all subscribers via webhooks. It reduces the load on your server and updates arrive without delay. Many feed readers and aggregators support WebSub, such as Feedly or NewsBlur. Microsub: the decoupled reader Microsub is the youngest standard and still a draft. Think of it as the infrastructure that ties everything together into a social reading application. There are 2 layers: a server that does the plumbing (managing subscriptions, fetching and parsing feeds, normalizing data) and a client that only renders the reading interface. This way clients compete on UX and your subscriptions are portable between them. Combined with Micropub to reply from the reader and Webmention to notify, it forms the complete architecture of the IndieWeb "social reader". Publishing strategy: POSSE, PESOS and backfeed Besides the principles and the protocols, the IndieWeb offers a mental model for coexisting with social networks without giving them your content: POSSE (Publish on your Own Site, Syndicate Elsewhere): publish on your site first and then push copies to the networks, each one with a link to the original. It is the recommended technique. Your friends keep reading you where they already are, you keep the canonical copy, and if the network shuts down or bans you tomorrow, you lose nothing. The term was coined by Tantek Çelik in 2012, and it is practiced by everyone from Cory Doctorow on Pluralistic to Molly White, who repopularized it in 2024. A nice detail from the wiki: linking to the original from the copy is also "internet aikido" against spammers who copy posts, because they copy the link that credits you too. PESOS (Publish Elsewhere, Syndicate to your Own Site): the reverse path, publishing on the silo and archiving a copy on your site afterwards. The wiki is honest about its advantages (silo apps are very polished, and it is asynchronous: if your website goes down, you can still publish), but considers it inferior: you publish subject to the silo's terms from the very first second, your copy is not the canonical one and you inherit its limits, like character caps or links wrapped in t.co. Backfeed: the reverse syndication of interactions. The likes, replies and reposts that your POSSE copies receive on the networks come back to your original post as webmentions. This way the full conversation is archived on your domain, safe from the next silo death. The reference service is Bridgy: it watches your copies on Mastodon, GitHub, Flickr, Reddit or Bluesky and sends you a webmention for each interaction. In short: you publish on your site, you syndicate to the silos with a link back, and the reactions come back home converted into webmentions. Relationship with the Fediverse, RSS and friends It may surprise you, but the IndieWeb does not live isolated from the Fediverse. Fun fact: protocols like Webmention, Micropub and WebSub came out of the same place, the W3C Social Web Working Group. The same group also gave birth to the famous ActivityPub, the protocol of the Fediverse. They are first cousins, although they follow different philosophies. The Fediverse federates servers: your identity is @user@instance, and if you don't run your own instance, you depend on someone else's. The wiki does not mince words: joining a Mastodon instance means going "from one silo, to a perhaps more open source-based and more open standards supporting silo, yet still dependent on another central org", with the aggravating factor of the admintax, the brutal cost of moderating and maintaining an instance. The IndieWeb federates websites: your identity is your domain and federation is just one more channel. However, they are not incompatible. Bridgy Fed is the bridge that converts your h-card, your h-entry and your webmentions to ActivityPub (and Bluesky's AT Protocol) and vice versa. The result is that your domain becomes a Fediverse account of the form @example.com@example.com where people can find and follow you from Mastodon, and replies come back to your post as backfeed. Mastodon understands that your profile and your posts live on your site. RSS/Atom feeds, however, are a different story, since the IndieWeb considers them formats with structural problems. The argument is the DRY principle: the content is already in your HTML, so maintaining a parallel XML copy is a "maintenance tax", a separate code path that can drift out of sync, weighs more (there are Atom files up to 4.5 times larger than their HTML equivalent) and offers a terrible experience when a human clicks on the feed link. Their alternative is h-feed: the HTML itself as the feed. But as so often happens, being technically superior does not mean being accepted. They themselves admit that very few readers support microformats. Therefore, they recommend h-feed for the IndieWeb and RSS/Atom for the rest of the planet. How to get started The Getting Started guide recommends these steps, in this order: Get your domain and use it as your primary identity online. A concrete tip they give: consider the registrar's whois privacy options, but only if you fully trust the provider, because ownership disputes get complicated if you are not listed as the legal owner. Set up hosting: a managed service if you are starting out (they mention GitHub Pages, Netlify, Neocities...), self-hosting if you know what you are doing. Create your pages: static site generator, handwritten HTML or a CMS, it doesn't matter. There is no official technology, and that is deliberate (the plurality principle). Syndicate to other platforms (POSSE) with links back to the original. Add microformats: rel="me" links to your profiles on the homepage and h-entry markup on your posts. Validate your work with IndieWebify.me, which checks your rel-me, your h-card and your h-entry step by step. Join the community: share what you build even if it is a single page, and document it on the wiki for the next person. For those who want a more gradual path there is IndieMark, a scale of levels, an orientation guide for developers. What I have implemented (and what I have not) I promised you at the beginning that I would tell you which pieces I implemented, which ones I discarded and why. Here is the list: ✅ Webmention, sending and receiving. Sending is automated with a daily task that notifies the external links of new articles, with a 24-hour margin to fix typos before telling anyone. I use the receiving side to include a list of references at the end of each article. However, I keep it separate from my comments, since those are managed by email. ✅ h-entry on every article and h-card on the homepage, verified with mf2py. ✅ rel="me" in the footer pointing to Mastodon, GitHub and Org Social, with its corresponding green checkmark. ❌ Micropub and IndieAuth: a conscious decision. My publishing interface is my editor and git; articles are versioned markdown. A publishing endpoint gives me nothing. ❌ WebSub: evaluated and discarded. Since my publishing is deliberately delayed by 24 hours, "real time" does not justify the complexity. ❌ h-feed: my article cards are reused in other contexts, such as the suggestions that appear inside each article, and marking them up would generate ambiguous h-entries for parsers. If I had designed the templates with this structure in mind, it would have been different. RSS covers that role just fine. But the advice that helped me the most is not technical, it is about philosophy and web design ethics. Scattered across the wiki, it is worth gold with or without the IndieWeb. My selection: Plain HTML is the most durable format. You can read the whole web without using JavaScript. Cool URIs don't change: design permalinks you can maintain forever. For example, I can change the slug of an article and it will still work. Gradual commitment, silo by silo: no exodus needed. Every type of content you start publishing on your own site first is a step forward in owning your data. Think about extreme longevity: the wiki seriously discusses what happens to your website when you die, from the "dead man's switch" that hands the keys to someone you trust, to the practical problem Peter Molnar sums up as: "who's going to pay for your domain if you're not?". And... have fun. An imperfect, weird personal website is worth more than a spotless template you are bored of maintaining.

blocks.html_en: Driven by curiosity, I decided to find out what the IndieWeb tag that kept showing up on some blogs and Mastodon toots was all about. I quickly discovered it was more than an empty concept or a feeling of nostalgia for the web of the 90s. It was a movement with concrete ideas, protocols and an active community! As I read through their wiki, I understood their position and the intelligence of their proposals more and more. Such was my enthusiasm that I decided to follow their advice and adopt the protocols that made sense for my site. After finishing the tests and adjustments, I can confirm that the experience has been very positive: I have learned a lot, the UX of my website has improved a bit more, and I enjoyed the process.

sentences.en: Driven by curiosity, I decided to find out what the IndieWeb tag that kept showing up on some blogs and Mastodon toots was all about.
sentences.zh: 在好奇心的驱使下，我决定找出一些博客和 Mastodon toots 上不断出现的 IndieWeb 标签到底是什么意思。

sentences.en: I quickly discovered it was more than an empty concept or a feeling of nostalgia for the web of the 90s.
sentences.zh: 我很快发现这不仅仅是一个空洞的概念，也不仅仅是对 90 年代网络的怀旧之情。

sentences.en: It was a movement with concrete ideas, protocols and an active community!
sentences.zh: 这是一场具有具体想法、协议和活跃社区的运动！

sentences.en: As I read through their wiki, I understood their position and the intelligence of their proposals more and more.
sentences.zh: 当我阅读他们的维基时，我越来越了解他们的立场和他们提案的智慧。

sentences.en: Such was my enthusiasm that I decided to follow their advice and adopt the protocols that made sense for my site.
sentences.zh: 由于我的热情，我决定听从他们的建议并采用对我的网站有意义的协议。

sentences.en: After finishing the tests and adjustments, I can confirm that the experience has been very positive: I have learned a lot, the UX of my website has improved a bit more, and I enjoyed the process.
sentences.zh: 完成测试和调整后，我可以确认体验非常积极：我学到了很多东西，我网站的用户体验得到了更多改善，而且我很享受这个过程。

blocks.html_en: That is why I decided to write this article, cleaning up my notes from these last few months. Maybe it will help someone else discover it, and along the way, improve the health of the web. And for the nosy ones, I will also tell you which pieces I implemented, which ones I discarded and why, and which pieces of advice turned out to be the most useful.

sentences.en: That is why I decided to write this article, cleaning up my notes from these last few months.
sentences.zh: 这就是为什么我决定写这篇文章，清理过去几个月的笔记。

sentences.en: Maybe it will help someone else discover it, and along the way, improve the health of the web.
sentences.zh: 也许它会帮助其他人发现它，并一路改善网络的健康状况。

sentences.en: And for the nosy ones, I will also tell you which pieces I implemented, which ones I discarded and why, and which pieces of advice turned out to be the most useful.
sentences.zh: 对于那些爱管闲事的人，我还会告诉你我实施了哪些部分，丢弃了哪些部分以及原因，以及哪些建议被证明是最有用的。

blocks.html_en: But first, we need to answer a fundamental question.

sentences.en: But first, we need to answer a fundamental question.
sentences.zh: 但首先，我们需要回答一个基本问题。

blocks.html_en: The IndieWeb defines itself as "a people-focused alternative to the corporate web". It does not propose a piece of software or a framework, but an ideological foundation. It deliberately embraces a plurality of approaches and projects. As its homepage says: "we are people-focused instead of project-focused".

sentences.en: The IndieWeb defines itself as "a people-focused alternative to the corporate web".
sentences.zh: IndieWeb 将自己定义为“以人为本的企业网络替代方案”。

sentences.en: It does not propose a piece of software or a framework, but an ideological foundation.
sentences.zh: 它提出的不是一个软件或一个框架，而是一个思想基础。

sentences.en: It deliberately embraces a plurality of approaches and projects.
sentences.zh: 它有意包含多种方法和项目。

sentences.en: As its homepage says: "we are people-focused instead of project-focused".
sentences.zh: 正如其主页所说：“我们以人为本，而不是以项目为中心”。

blocks.html_en: It all started in 2010, when Aaron Parecki and Tantek Çelik attended the Federated Social Web Summit in Portland. They left with the feeling that a different approach was needed: fewer protocols and more creators. In 2011 the first IndieWebCamp was held in Portland, and they have been celebrated every year around the world ever since, along with the Homebrew Website Club, meetups where people get together to improve their personal websites.

sentences.en: It all started in 2010, when Aaron Parecki and Tantek Çelik attended the Federated Social Web Summit in Portland.
sentences.zh: 这一切都始于 2010 年，当时 Aaron Parecki 和 Tantek Çelik 参加了在波特兰举行的联邦社交网络峰会。

sentences.en: They left with the feeling that a different approach was needed: fewer protocols and more creators.
sentences.zh: 他们离开时感觉需要一种不同的方法：更少的协议和更多的创建者。

sentences.en: In 2011 the first IndieWebCamp was held in Portland, and they have been celebrated every year around the world ever since, along with the Homebrew Website Club, meetups where people get together to improve their personal websites.
sentences.zh: 2011 年，第一届 IndieWebCamp 在波特兰举行，此后每年都会在世界各地庆祝，此外还有 Homebrew 网站俱乐部，人们聚在一起改进他们的个人网站。

blocks.html_en: The 3 pillars that define it are:

sentences.en: The 3 pillars that define it are:
sentences.zh: 定义它的三大支柱是：

blocks.html_en: • Your content is yours: when you post something on the web, it should belong to you, not a corporation. Too many companies have shut down and taken their users' data with them.

sentences.en: Your content is yours: when you post something on the web, it should belong to you, not a corporation.
sentences.zh: 您的内容是您的：当您在网络上发布某些内容时，它应该属于您，而不是公司。

sentences.en: Too many companies have shut down and taken their users' data with them.
sentences.zh: 太多的公司已经关闭并带走了用户的数据。

blocks.html_en: • You are better connected: your articles can be distributed to any platform, not just one, and responses and likes from other services can come back to your site so you have everything in one place.

sentences.en: You are better connected: your articles can be distributed to any platform, not just one, and responses and likes from other services can come back to your site so you have everything in one place.
sentences.zh: 您可以更好地连接：您的文章可以分发到任何平台，而不仅仅是一个平台，来自其他服务的回复和点赞可以返回到您的网站，因此您可以将所有内容集中在一处。

blocks.html_en: • You are in control: you can post anything you want, in any format you want, with readable and permanent URLs that will always work.

sentences.en: You are in control: you can post anything you want, in any format you want, with readable and permanent URLs that will always work.
sentences.zh: 您尽在掌控：您可以以任何您想要的格式发布任何您想要的内容，并且具有始终有效的可读且永久的 URL。

blocks.html_en: So we could say it is a community of personal, independent websites that share these commitments.

sentences.en: So we could say it is a community of personal, independent websites that share these commitments.
sentences.zh: 因此，我们可以说它是一个由共享这些承诺的个人独立网站组成的社区。

blocks.html_en: That is also why it does not forbid you from using social networks, but it does fight against walled gardens.

sentences.en: That is also why it does not forbid you from using social networks, but it does fight against walled gardens.
sentences.zh: 这也是为什么它不禁止你使用社交网络，但它确实与围墙花园作斗争。

blocks.html_en: The enemy has a name: the silo

sentences.en: The enemy has a name: the silo
sentences.zh: 敌人有一个名字：筒仓

blocks.html_en: The entire IndieWeb vocabulary is built in opposition to one concept: the silo, also called a walled garden. The wiki defines it as a centralized website, typically owned by a for-profit corporation, that claims some rights over the content you contribute and restricts access in some way. Its characteristics:

sentences.en: The entire IndieWeb vocabulary is built in opposition to one concept: the silo, also called a walled garden.
sentences.zh: 整个 IndieWeb 词汇都是与一个概念相对立的：筒仓，也称为围墙花园。

sentences.en: The wiki defines it as a centralized website, typically owned by a for-profit corporation, that claims some rights over the content you contribute and restricts access in some way.
sentences.zh: 维基将其定义为一个集中式网站，通常由营利性公司拥有，该网站对您贡献的内容拥有某些权利，并以某种方式限制访问。

sentences.en: Its characteristics:
sentences.zh: 其特点：

blocks.html_en: • They require you to create a site-specific account to participate.

sentences.en: They require you to create a site-specific account to participate.
sentences.zh: 他们要求您创建一个特定于站点的帐户才能参与。

blocks.html_en: • They only let you interact with other accounts on the same site.

sentences.en: They only let you interact with other accounts on the same site.
sentences.zh: 它们只允许您与同一网站上的其他帐户进行交互。

blocks.html_en: • And they typically add: restrictive terms of service, license claims over what you create inside, walls that prevent indexing, or barriers to importing and exporting your content.

sentences.en: And they typically add: restrictive terms of service, license claims over what you create inside, walls that prevent indexing, or barriers to importing and exporting your content.
sentences.zh: 他们通常会添加：限制性服务条款、对您在内部创建的内容的许可声明、阻止索引的墙壁或导入和导出内容的障碍。

blocks.html_en: Why is this a problem? Because silos die, and when they die they take your content with them. The site-deaths page ("Where incredible journeys end", a nod to the corporate euphemism our incredible journey) keeps a devastating chronology:

sentences.en: Why is this a problem?
sentences.zh: 为什么这是一个问题？

sentences.en: Because silos die, and when they die they take your content with them.
sentences.zh: 因为筒仓会消亡，当它们消亡时，您的内容也会随之消失。

sentences.en: The site-deaths page ("Where incredible journeys end", a nod to the corporate euphemism our incredible journey) keeps a devastating chronology:
sentences.zh: 网站死亡页面（“不可思议的旅程结束的地方”，这是对我们不可思议的旅程的企业委婉说法）保留了毁灭性的年表：

blocks.html_en: • GeoCities: Yahoo shut it down on October 26, 2009. 23 million pages gone.

sentences.en: GeoCities: Yahoo shut it down on October 26, 2009.
sentences.zh: GeoCities：雅虎于 2009 年 10 月 26 日将其关闭。

sentences.en: 23 million pages gone.
sentences.zh: 2300 万页消失了。

blocks.html_en: • MySpace: in 2019, during a server migration, it lost all the music uploaded during its first 12 years, more than 50 million songs from 14 million artists.

sentences.en: MySpace: in 2019, during a server migration, it lost all the music uploaded during its first 12 years, more than 50 million songs from 14 million artists.
sentences.zh: MySpace：2019 年，在服务器迁移过程中，它丢失了前 12 年上传的所有音乐，来自 1400 万艺术家的超过 5000 万首歌曲。

blocks.html_en: • Google+: shut down in April 2019.

sentences.en: Google+: shut down in April 2019.
sentences.zh: Google+：于 2019 年 4 月关闭。

blocks.html_en: • Posterous, FriendFeed, Vine, Yahoo Groups, TinyLetter, Cohost... the list keeps growing, and it even has sections for "upcoming deaths" and for acquisitions, which tend to foreshadow them.

sentences.en: Posterous, FriendFeed, Vine, Yahoo Groups, TinyLetter, Cohost...
sentences.zh: Posterous、FriendFeed、Vine、雅虎群组、TinyLetter、Cohost...

sentences.en: the list keeps growing, and it even has sections for "upcoming deaths" and for acquisitions, which tend to foreshadow them.
sentences.zh: 该列表不断增长，甚至有“即将死亡”和收购的部分，这往往预示着它们。

blocks.html_en: The silo does not even need to die: the web is fragile by default. According to a 2024 Pew Research study, 38% of the web pages that existed in 2013 were no longer accessible a decade later.

sentences.en: The silo does not even need to die: the web is fragile by default.
sentences.zh: 筒仓甚至不需要消亡：网络默认情况下是脆弱的。

sentences.en: According to a 2024 Pew Research study, 38% of the web pages that existed in 2013 were no longer accessible a decade later.
sentences.zh: 根据皮尤研究中心 2024 年的一项研究，2013 年存在的网页中有 38% 在 10 年后不再可访问。

blocks.html_en: The IndieWeb's conclusion is not "don't use social networks". It is more subtle: use whatever you want, but make sure the canonical copy of your content lives on a domain you control.

sentences.en: The IndieWeb's conclusion is not "don't use social networks".
sentences.zh: IndieWeb 的结论并不是“不要使用社交网络”。

sentences.en: It is more subtle: use whatever you want, but make sure the canonical copy of your content lives on a domain you control.
sentences.zh: 它更微妙：使用您想要的任何内容，但要确保内容的规范副本位于您控制的域中。

blocks.html_en: That is why it defines a set of principles to fight against the fragility of the network.

sentences.en: That is why it defines a set of principles to fight against the fragility of the network.
sentences.zh: 这就是为什么它定义了一套原则来对抗网络的脆弱性。

blocks.html_en: The principles

sentences.en: The principles
sentences.zh: 原则

blocks.html_en: The community is guided by 11 principles:

sentences.en: The community is guided by 11 principles:
sentences.zh: 该社区遵循 11 条原则：

blocks.html_en: • Own your data: your content, your metadata and your identity live under your domain, and you retain access to them over time.

sentences.en: Own your data: your content, your metadata and your identity live under your domain, and you retain access to them over time.
sentences.zh: 拥有您的数据：您的内容、元数据和身份都位于您的域下，并且随着时间的推移，您保留对它们的访问权限。

blocks.html_en: • Use and publish visible data: for humans first, machines second. No parallel APIs if the HTML can carry the data.

sentences.en: Use and publish visible data: for humans first, machines second.
sentences.zh: 使用和发布可见数据：首先是人类，其次是机器。

sentences.en: No parallel APIs if the HTML can carry the data.
sentences.zh: 如果 HTML 可以承载数据，则无需并行 API。

blocks.html_en: • Make what you need: build tools for yourself, not for some hypothetical user. "If you design for some hypothetical user, they may not actually exist; if you make for yourself, you actually do exist."

sentences.en: Make what you need: build tools for yourself, not for some hypothetical user.
sentences.zh: 制作您需要的东西：为自己构建工具，而不是为某个假设的用户构建工具。

sentences.en: "If you design for some hypothetical user, they may not actually exist; if you make for yourself, you actually do exist."
sentences.zh: “如果你为某个假设的用户设计，他们可能实际上并不存在；如果你为自己设计，那么你确实存在。”

blocks.html_en: • Use what you make: use what you build every day. "If you aren't depending on it, why should anybody else?"

sentences.en: Use what you make: use what you build every day.
sentences.zh: 使用你所做的：使用你每天构建的东西。

sentences.en: "If you aren't depending on it, why should anybody else?"
sentences.zh: “如果你都不依赖它，为什么其他人要依赖它呢？”

blocks.html_en: • Document your stuff: you already have a place to speak your mind, use it to document your processes, ideas and code. You help others and your future self.

sentences.en: Document your stuff: you already have a place to speak your mind, use it to document your processes, ideas and code.
sentences.zh: 记录你的东西：你已经有了一个表达你的想法的地方，用它来记录你的流程、想法和代码。

sentences.en: You help others and your future self.
sentences.zh: 你帮助别人和未来的自己。

blocks.html_en: • Open source your stuff: not mandatory, but it helps others get onto the independent web faster.

sentences.en: Open source your stuff: not mandatory, but it helps others get onto the independent web faster.
sentences.zh: 开源你的东西：不是强制性的，但它可以帮助其他人更快地进入独立网络。

blocks.html_en: • UX before protocols: user experience first, and then the simplest, most minimal protocol sufficient to support it, and nothing more. They sum it up as "UX before plumbing".

sentences.en: UX before protocols: user experience first, and then the simplest, most minimal protocol sufficient to support it, and nothing more.
sentences.zh: UX 先于协议：用户体验第一，然后是足以支持它的最简单、最小的协议，仅此而已。

sentences.en: They sum it up as "UX before plumbing".
sentences.zh: 他们将其总结为“先于管道的用户体验”。

blocks.html_en: • Modularity: small, loosely coupled pieces, so you don't depend on a specific device, language or platform.

sentences.en: Modularity: small, loosely coupled pieces, so you don't depend on a specific device, language or platform.
sentences.zh: 模块化：小的、松散耦合的部分，因此您不依赖于特定的设备、语言或平台。

blocks.html_en: • Longevity: build for the long web. "If human society is able to preserve ancient papyrus, Victorian photographs and dinosaur bones, we should be able to build web technology that doesn't require us to destroy everything we've done every few years in the name of progress."

sentences.en: Longevity: build for the long web.
sentences.zh: 长寿：为长网络而构建。

sentences.en: "If human society is able to preserve ancient papyrus, Victorian photographs and dinosaur bones, we should be able to build web technology that doesn't require us to destroy everything we've done every few years in the name of progress."
sentences.zh: “如果人类社会能够保存古代纸莎草纸、维多利亚时代的照片和恐龙骨骼，我们就应该能够建立网络技术，而不需要我们每隔几年以进步的名义摧毁我们所做的一切。”

blocks.html_en: • Plurality: deliberately encouraging diverse approaches makes the community more resilient than any monoculture.

sentences.en: Plurality: deliberately encouraging diverse approaches makes the community more resilient than any monoculture.
sentences.zh: 多元化：刻意鼓励多元化的方法使社区比任何单一文化都更具弹性。

blocks.html_en: • And above all, have fun: remember the web of the 90s, GeoCities, loud backgrounds and animated GIFs. "It may have been ugly and badly coded but it was fun. Keep the web weird and interesting."

sentences.en: And above all, have fun: remember the web of the 90s, GeoCities, loud backgrounds and animated GIFs.
sentences.zh: 最重要的是，玩得开心：记住 90 年代的网络、GeoCities、响亮的背景和动画 GIF。

sentences.en: "It may have been ugly and badly coded but it was fun.
sentences.zh: “它可能很丑陋并且编码很糟糕，但很有趣。

sentences.en: Keep the web weird and interesting."
sentences.zh: 保持网络怪异和有趣。”

blocks.html_en: The numbering is just for reference, not a priority. The community does not demand that you fulfill them all, but it does ask you to keep them in mind.

sentences.en: The numbering is just for reference, not a priority.
sentences.zh: 编号仅供参考，并非优先级。

sentences.en: The community does not demand that you fulfill them all, but it does ask you to keep them in mind.
sentences.zh: 社区并不要求你满足所有这些要求，但它确实要求你牢记它们。

blocks.html_en: But the IndieWeb does not live on principles alone. There is a set of standards that helps make your sites more open, interoperable and resistant to disappearing.

sentences.en: But the IndieWeb does not live on principles alone.
sentences.zh: 但 IndieWeb 并不仅仅依靠原则生存。

sentences.en: There is a set of standards that helps make your sites more open, interoperable and resistant to disappearing.
sentences.zh: 有一组标准可以帮助您的网站更加开放、具有互操作性并防止消失。

blocks.html_en: The technical part

sentences.en: The technical part
sentences.zh: 技术部分

blocks.html_en: The IndieWeb does not invent a platform; it defines a handful of small standards that compose with each other. The official index orders them by age and breadth of implementation.

sentences.en: The IndieWeb does not invent a platform; it defines a handful of small standards that compose with each other.
sentences.zh: IndieWeb 并没有发明一个平台，而是创造了一个平台。它定义了一些相互组合的小标准。

sentences.en: The official index orders them by age and breadth of implementation.
sentences.zh: 官方索引按年龄和实施广度对它们进行排序。

blocks.html_en: Let's go one by one.

sentences.en: Let's go one by one.
sentences.zh: 我们一一来说吧。

blocks.html_en: The starting point: your domain

sentences.en: The starting point: your domain
sentences.zh: 起点：您的域

blocks.html_en: It is not a protocol, but it is the prerequisite for everything else: your own domain used as your primary identity online. It is the first step of the Getting Started guide and the bare minimum the community considers necessary to be "on" the IndieWeb. If tomorrow you change hosting or CMS while keeping the domain, all your links, readers and search rankings survive the move.

sentences.en: It is not a protocol, but it is the prerequisite for everything else: your own domain used as your primary identity online.
sentences.zh: 它不是一个协议，但它是其他一切的先决条件：您自己的域用作您的主要在线身份。

sentences.en: It is the first step of the Getting Started guide and the bare minimum the community considers necessary to be "on" the IndieWeb.
sentences.zh: 这是入门指南的第一步，也是社区认为“在”IndieWeb 上所需的最低限度。

sentences.en: If tomorrow you change hosting or CMS while keeping the domain, all your links, readers and search rankings survive the move.
sentences.zh: 如果明天您在保留域名的情况下更改托管或 CMS，您的所有链接、读者和搜索排名都会在迁移后继续存在。

blocks.html_en: microformats2: your HTML is your API

sentences.en: microformats2: your HTML is your API
sentences.zh: 微格式2：您的 HTML 就是您的 API

blocks.html_en: microformats2 solves one problem: making your content machine-readable without publishing parallel files or building an API.

sentences.en: microformats2 solves one problem: making your content machine-readable without publishing parallel files or building an API.
sentences.zh: microformats2 解决了一个问题：使您的内容可供机器读取，而无需发布并行文件或构建 API。

blocks.html_en: The implementation is elegant, since it uses CSS classes that you add to the HTML you already have. The prefixes indicate the data type: h-* for roots, p-* for plain text, u-* for URLs, dt-* for dates and e-* for embedded HTML. The two essential vocabularies:

sentences.en: The implementation is elegant, since it uses CSS classes that you add to the HTML you already have.
sentences.zh: 该实现非常优雅，因为它使用您添加到已有 HTML 中的 CSS 类。

sentences.en: The prefixes indicate the data type: h-* for roots, p-* for plain text, u-* for URLs, dt-* for dates and e-* for embedded HTML.
sentences.zh: 前缀表示数据类型：h-* 表示根，p-* 表示纯文本，u-* 表示 URL，dt-* 表示日期，e-* 表示嵌入 HTML。

sentences.en: The two essential vocabularies:
sentences.zh: 两个基本词汇：

blocks.html_en: h-card is your identity: the online equivalent of a business card. With a minimum of name, URL and photo on your homepage, readers show your profile next to your posts and applications recognize you. It works like a domain-based Gravatar instead of an email-based one:

sentences.en: h-card is your identity: the online equivalent of a business card.
sentences.zh: h-card 是您的身份：相当于在线名片。

sentences.en: With a minimum of name, URL and photo on your homepage, readers show your profile next to your posts and applications recognize you.
sentences.zh: 只需在主页上显示最少的姓名、URL 和照片，读者就会在您的帖子旁边显示您的个人资料，并且应用程序会识别您。

sentences.en: It works like a domain-based Gravatar instead of an email-based one:
sentences.zh: 它的工作原理类似于基于域名的 Gravatar，而不是基于电子邮件的 Gravatar：

blocks.html_en: h-entry is the unit of content: the markup of a post. The wiki calls it "the key building block for the indieweb":

sentences.en: h-entry is the unit of content: the markup of a post.
sentences.zh: h-entry 是内容的单位：帖子的标记。

sentences.en: The wiki calls it "the key building block for the indieweb":
sentences.zh: 维基百科称其为“独立网络的关键构建模块”：

blocks.html_en: There is also h-feed, which groups several h-entry elements to turn your listing page into a feed you can subscribe to straight from the HTML.

sentences.en: There is also h-feed, which groups several h-entry elements to turn your listing page into a feed you can subscribe to straight from the HTML.
sentences.zh: 还有 h-feed，它将几个 h-entry 元素组合在一起，将您的列表页面变成可以直接从 HTML 订阅的 feed。

blocks.html_en: The wiki sums it up in a phrase I love:

sentences.en: The wiki sums it up in a phrase I love:
sentences.zh: 维基用我喜欢的一句话总结了这一点：

blocks.html_en: Your website is your API

sentences.en: Your website is your API
sentences.zh: 您的网站就是您的 API

blocks.html_en: Your website is your API

sentences.en: Your website is your API
sentences.zh: 您的网站就是您的 API

blocks.html_en: For reading, microformats; for writing, Micropub (we'll get there).

sentences.en: For reading, microformats; for writing, Micropub (we'll get there).
sentences.zh: 用于阅读、微格式；对于写作，Micropub（我们会到达那里）。

blocks.html_en: rel-me is the simplest piece and the one with the most immediate effect. An attribute on a link that says "the destination of this link represents the same person as the current page":

sentences.en: rel-me is the simplest piece and the one with the most immediate effect.
sentences.zh: rel-me 是最简单的作品，也是效果最直接的作品。

sentences.en: An attribute on a link that says "the destination of this link represents the same person as the current page":
sentences.zh: 链接上的一个属性，表示“此链接的目标代表与当前页面相同的人”：

blocks.html_en: Verification requires reciprocity: your website links to the profile and the profile links back to your website, both with rel="me". With that you get distributed identity verification, with no central authority involved. It is exactly the mechanism behind Mastodon's green verified checkmark: if your page links to your profile with rel-me and your profile links back, Mastodon shows your domain as verified. It is also supported by Threads, PixelFed, GitHub, Keybase and Wikipedia.

sentences.en: Verification requires reciprocity: your website links to the profile and the profile links back to your website, both with rel="me".
sentences.zh: 验证需要互惠：您的网站链接到个人资料，个人资料链接回您的网站，两者都带有 rel="me"。

sentences.en: With that you get distributed identity verification, with no central authority involved.
sentences.zh: 这样，您就可以获得分布式身份验证，无需涉及中央机构。

sentences.en: It is exactly the mechanism behind Mastodon's green verified checkmark: if your page links to your profile with rel-me and your profile links back, Mastodon shows your domain as verified.
sentences.zh: 这正是 Mastodon 绿色验证复选标记背后的机制：如果您的页面使用 rel-me 链接到您的个人资料，并且您的个人资料链接回来，Mastodon 会显示您的域名已验证。

sentences.en: It is also supported by Threads, PixelFed, GitHub, Keybase and Wikipedia.
sentences.zh: 它还受到 Threads、PixelFed、GitHub、Keybase 和 Wikipedia 的支持。

blocks.html_en: On top of rel-me sits RelMeAuth: authenticating on services with your personal URL, delegating the identity proof to an OAuth provider (like GitHub) that your homepage links to. It is the foundation of services like IndieLogin.

sentences.en: On top of rel-me sits RelMeAuth: authenticating on services with your personal URL, delegating the identity proof to an OAuth provider (like GitHub) that your homepage links to.
sentences.zh: RelMeAuth 位于 rel-me 之上：使用您的个人 URL 对服务进行身份验证，将身份证明委托给您主页链接到的 OAuth 提供商（如 GitHub）。

sentences.en: It is the foundation of services like IndieLogin.
sentences.zh: 它是 IndieLogin 等服务的基础。

blocks.html_en: Webmention: conversations between sites

sentences.en: Webmention: conversations between sites
sentences.zh: Webmention：站点之间的对话

blocks.html_en: Webmention is the star standard, a W3C Recommendation since January 12, 2017, and the modern successor of Pingback. It solves conversations between sites: comments, likes, replies and reposts from web to web, with no platform in between. Many people use it as a replacement for Disqus.

sentences.en: Webmention is the star standard, a W3C Recommendation since January 12, 2017, and the modern successor of Pingback.
sentences.zh: Webmention 是明星标准，自 2017 年 1 月 12 日起成为 W3C 推荐标准，也是 Pingback 的现代继承者。

sentences.en: It solves conversations between sites: comments, likes, replies and reposts from web to web, with no platform in between.
sentences.zh: 它解决了网站之间的对话：从网络到网络的评论、点赞、回复和转发，中间没有平台。

sentences.en: Many people use it as a replacement for Disqus.
sentences.zh: 许多人用它作为 Disqus 的替代品。

blocks.html_en: The flow is deliberately simple:

sentences.en: The flow is deliberately simple:
sentences.zh: 流程故意简单：

blocks.html_en: • I write a post that links to an article of yours.

sentences.en: I write a post that links to an article of yours.
sentences.zh: 我写了一篇文章，链接到您的一篇文章。

blocks.html_en: • My server visits your article and looks for your endpoint: an HTTP header Link: <...>; rel="webmention" or a <link rel="webmention"> in the HTML.

sentences.en: My server visits your article and looks for your endpoint: an HTTP header Link: <...>; rel="webmention" or a <link rel="webmention"> in the HTML.
sentences.zh: 我的服务器访问您的文章并查找您的端点：HTTP 标头链接：<...>; HTML 中的 rel="webmention" 或 <link rel="webmention">。

blocks.html_en: • It sends a POST with only two parameters: source (my post) and target (yours).

sentences.en: It sends a POST with only two parameters: source (my post) and target (yours).
sentences.zh: 它发送一个仅包含两个参数的 POST：源（我的帖子）和目标（您的）。

blocks.html_en: • Your server verifies the mention: the specification requires downloading the source and checking that it really contains a link to the target. Without that verification, anyone could fabricate fake mentions.

sentences.en: Your server verifies the mention: the specification requires downloading the source and checking that it really contains a link to the target.
sentences.zh: 您的服务器验证提及：规范要求下载源并检查它是否确实包含指向目标的链接。

sentences.en: Without that verification, anyone could fabricate fake mentions.
sentences.zh: 如果没有这种验证，任何人都可以伪造虚假提及。

blocks.html_en: • Once verified, your site decides what to do with it. And here is where microformats comes in: by parsing the h-entry of the source you know whether it is a reply (u-in-reply-to), a like (u-like-of) or a repost (u-repost-of), and the author's h-card lets you display their name and photo like in any comment section.

sentences.en: Once verified, your site decides what to do with it.
sentences.zh: 验证后，您的网站将决定如何处理它。

sentences.en: And here is where microformats comes in: by parsing the h-entry of the source you know whether it is a reply (u-in-reply-to), a like (u-like-of) or a repost (u-repost-of), and the author's h-card lets you display their name and photo like in any comment section.
sentences.zh: 这就是微格式的用武之地：通过解析源的 h-entry，您可以知道它是回复 (u-in-reply-to)、点赞 (u-like-of) 还是转发 (u-repost-of)，而作者的 h-card 可以让您像在任何评论部分一样显示他们的姓名和照片。

blocks.html_en: If this sounds like a social network to you, that's because it is! Every site is a node in the network, and the links between them form the social graph.

sentences.en: If this sounds like a social network to you, that's because it is!
sentences.zh: 如果您觉得这听起来像一个社交网络，那是因为它确实如此！

sentences.en: Every site is a node in the network, and the links between them form the social graph.
sentences.zh: 每个站点都是网络中的一个节点，它们之间的链接形成社交图谱。

blocks.html_en: It is not a perfect system, since it suffers from the same problems as any other decentralized network, which is why there are a couple of extensions that attack its weak points:

sentences.en: It is not a perfect system, since it suffers from the same problems as any other decentralized network, which is why there are a couple of extensions that attack its weak points:
sentences.zh: 它不是一个完美的系统，因为它与任何其他去中心化网络都存在同样的问题，这就是为什么有一些扩展可以攻击它的弱点：

blocks.html_en: • Vouch, against spam: the webmention carries a third parameter with the URL of a "voucher", a site you already know that links to the sender's domain. It shifts the filtering cost from the receiver to the sender.

sentences.en: Vouch, against spam: the webmention carries a third parameter with the URL of a "voucher", a site you already know that links to the sender's domain.
sentences.zh: 凭证，防止垃圾邮件：网络提及带有第三个参数，其中包含“凭证”的 URL，该网站是您已知的链接到发件人域的网站。

sentences.en: It shifts the filtering cost from the receiver to the sender.
sentences.zh: 它将过滤成本从接收者转移到发送者。

blocks.html_en: • Salmention, to propagate threads: if someone replies to a comment on my post, the original post finds out by re-sending update webmentions to everyone involved.

sentences.en: Salmention, to propagate threads: if someone replies to a comment on my post, the original post finds out by re-sending update webmentions to everyone involved.
sentences.zh: Salmention，传播主题：如果有人回复我帖子的评论，原始帖子会通过向所有相关人员重新发送更新网络提及来发现。

blocks.html_en: You don't escape spam or moderation. However, it is a good foundation for building a distributed comment system.

sentences.en: You don't escape spam or moderation.
sentences.zh: 您无法逃脱垃圾邮件或审核。

sentences.en: However, it is a good foundation for building a distributed comment system.
sentences.zh: 然而，它是构建分布式评论系统的良好基础。

blocks.html_en: What if your website has no backend? You are not left out: webmention.io receives webmentions on your behalf. You add a <link> in your HTML pointing to the service and it gives you an API to query and display them. It is exactly what you need if you use a static site generator like Hugo, Jekyll or Eleventy.

sentences.en: What if your website has no backend?
sentences.zh: 如果您的网站没有后端怎么办？

sentences.en: You are not left out: webmention.io receives webmentions on your behalf.
sentences.zh: 您不会被排除在外：webmention.io 代表您接收网络提及。

sentences.en: You add a <link> in your HTML pointing to the service and it gives you an API to query and display them.
sentences.zh: 您在 HTML 中添加一个指向该服务的 <link>，它为您提供了一个 API 来查询和显示它们。

sentences.en: It is exactly what you need if you use a static site generator like Hugo, Jekyll or Eleventy.
sentences.zh: 如果您使用 Hugo、Jekyll 或 Eleventy 等静态站点生成器，这正是您所需要的。

blocks.html_en: IndieAuth: your domain as your login

sentences.en: IndieAuth: your domain as your login
sentences.zh: IndieAuth：您的域名作为您的登录名

blocks.html_en: IndieAuth answers the question: what if your identity for signing in were your URL, instead of "you on Google" or "you on Facebook"? Technically it is OAuth 2.0, the standard for authentication and authorization. Both users and applications are identified by URLs, which removes the need for prior client registration ("IndieAuth uses DNS as a replacement for client registration"), and PKCE is mandatory (a security mechanism that prevents an attacker from stealing the access token).

sentences.en: IndieAuth answers the question: what if your identity for signing in were your URL, instead of "you on Google" or "you on Facebook"?
sentences.zh: IndieAuth 回答了这个问题：如果您的登录身份是您的 URL，而不是“您在 Google 上”或“您在 Facebook 上”，该怎么办？

sentences.en: Technically it is OAuth 2.0, the standard for authentication and authorization.
sentences.zh: 从技术上讲，它是 OAuth 2.0，身份验证和授权的标准。

sentences.en: Both users and applications are identified by URLs, which removes the need for prior client registration ("IndieAuth uses DNS as a replacement for client registration"), and PKCE is mandatory (a security mechanism that prevents an attacker from stealing the access token).
sentences.zh: 用户和应用程序都通过 URL 进行标识，这消除了事先客户端注册的需要（“IndieAuth 使用 DNS 作为客户端注册的替代品”），并且 PKCE 是强制性的（一种防止攻击者窃取访问令牌的安全机制）。

blocks.html_en: The flow in a nutshell: you type your domain in the login form, the service fetches your page and discovers your authorization server via rel="indieauth-metadata", redirects you there, you authenticate however you prefer (password, email, RelMeAuth), and the service receives confirmation that you control that URL. It is a living standard that the community considers stable.

sentences.en: The flow in a nutshell: you type your domain in the login form, the service fetches your page and discovers your authorization server via rel="indieauth-metadata", redirects you there, you authenticate however you prefer (password, email, RelMeAuth), and the service receives confirmation that you control that URL.
sentences.zh: 简而言之，流程是：您在登录表单中输入您的域名，服务获取您的页面并通过 rel="indieauth-metadata" 发现您的授权服务器，将您重定向到那里，您可以根据自己的喜好进行身份验证（密码、电子邮件、RelMeAuth），并且服务会收到您控制该 URL 的确认信息。

sentences.en: It is a living standard that the community considers stable.
sentences.zh: 这是社会认为稳定的生活水平。

blocks.html_en: Micropub: publish from any client

sentences.en: Micropub: publish from any client
sentences.zh: Micropub：从任何客户端发布

blocks.html_en: Micropub, a W3C Recommendation since May 2017, separates the publishing interface from your site's software: any application (web, iOS, Android) can create, edit and delete posts on your domain. It replaces the old MetaWeblog and AtomPub, which depended on sharing your password, with OAuth tokens obtained via IndieAuth.

sentences.en: Micropub, a W3C Recommendation since May 2017, separates the publishing interface from your site's software: any application (web, iOS, Android) can create, edit and delete posts on your domain.
sentences.zh: Micropub 自 2017 年 5 月起成为 W3C 推荐标准，它将发布界面与您网站的软件分开：任何应用程序（Web、iOS、Android）都可以在您的域上创建、编辑和删除帖子。

sentences.en: It replaces the old MetaWeblog and AtomPub, which depended on sharing your password, with OAuth tokens obtained via IndieAuth.
sentences.zh: 它使用通过 IndieAuth 获得的 OAuth 令牌取代了旧的 MetaWeblog 和 AtomPub（它们依赖于共享您的密码）。

blocks.html_en: The beautiful part is its vocabulary: it does not invent one, it is serialized microformats. Creating a post is a POST with h=entry and the same h-entry properties:

sentences.en: The beautiful part is its vocabulary: it does not invent one, it is serialized microformats.
sentences.zh: 它的美妙之处在于它的词汇：它不是发明词汇，而是序列化的微格式。

sentences.en: Creating a post is a POST with h=entry and the same h-entry properties:
sentences.zh: 创建帖子是一个带有 h=entry 和相同 h-entry 属性的 POST：

blocks.html_en: If you are already used to working with REST APIs, it will feel very natural.

sentences.en: If you are already used to working with REST APIs, it will feel very natural.
sentences.zh: 如果您已经习惯使用 REST API，那么这会感觉非常自然。

blocks.html_en: WebSub: real-time feeds

sentences.en: WebSub: real-time feeds
sentences.zh: WebSub：实时提要

blocks.html_en: WebSub, formerly known as PubSubHubbub, a W3C Recommendation since January 2018, removes feed polling: instead of a thousand readers asking your server every half hour whether there is something new, you notify a hub when you publish and the hub instantly notifies all subscribers via webhooks. It reduces the load on your server and updates arrive without delay.

sentences.en: WebSub, formerly known as PubSubHubbub, a W3C Recommendation since January 2018, removes feed polling: instead of a thousand readers asking your server every half hour whether there is something new, you notify a hub when you publish and the hub instantly notifies all subscribers via webhooks.
sentences.zh: WebSub（以前称为 PubSubHubbub）是自 2018 年 1 月起成为 W3C 推荐标准，它删除了 Feed 轮询：不再有一千名读者每半小时询问您的服务器是否有新内容，而是您在发布时通知中心，中心会立即通过 Webhooks 通知所有订阅者。

sentences.en: It reduces the load on your server and updates arrive without delay.
sentences.zh: 它减少了服务器的负载并且更新立即到达。

blocks.html_en: Many feed readers and aggregators support WebSub, such as Feedly or NewsBlur.

sentences.en: Many feed readers and aggregators support WebSub, such as Feedly or NewsBlur.
sentences.zh: 许多提要阅读器和聚合器都支持 WebSub，例如 Feedly 或 NewsBlur。

blocks.html_en: Microsub: the decoupled reader

sentences.en: Microsub: the decoupled reader
sentences.zh: Microsub：解耦阅读器

blocks.html_en: Microsub is the youngest standard and still a draft. Think of it as the infrastructure that ties everything together into a social reading application.

sentences.en: Microsub is the youngest standard and still a draft.
sentences.zh: Microsub 是最年轻的标准，目前仍处于草案阶段。

sentences.en: Think of it as the infrastructure that ties everything together into a social reading application.
sentences.zh: 将其视为将所有内容连接到社交阅读应用程序中的基础设施。

blocks.html_en: There are 2 layers: a server that does the plumbing (managing subscriptions, fetching and parsing feeds, normalizing data) and a client that only renders the reading interface. This way clients compete on UX and your subscriptions are portable between them. Combined with Micropub to reply from the reader and Webmention to notify, it forms the complete architecture of the IndieWeb "social reader".

sentences.en: There are 2 layers: a server that does the plumbing (managing subscriptions, fetching and parsing feeds, normalizing data) and a client that only renders the reading interface.
sentences.zh: 有 2 层：执行管道操作（管理订阅、获取和解析提要、规范化数据）的服务器和仅呈现阅读界面的客户端。

sentences.en: This way clients compete on UX and your subscriptions are portable between them.
sentences.zh: 这样，客户就可以在用户体验上进行竞争，并且您的订阅可以在他们之间移植。

sentences.en: Combined with Micropub to reply from the reader and Webmention to notify, it forms the complete architecture of the IndieWeb "social reader".
sentences.zh: 结合Micropub的读者回复和Webmention的通知，构成了IndieWeb“社交阅读器”的完整架构。

blocks.html_en: Publishing strategy: POSSE, PESOS and backfeed

sentences.en: Publishing strategy: POSSE, PESOS and backfeed
sentences.zh: 发布策略：POSSE、PESOS 和反馈

blocks.html_en: Besides the principles and the protocols, the IndieWeb offers a mental model for coexisting with social networks without giving them your content:

sentences.en: Besides the principles and the protocols, the IndieWeb offers a mental model for coexisting with social networks without giving them your content:
sentences.zh: 除了原则和协议之外，IndieWeb 还提供了一种与社交网络共存的心理模型，而无需向其提供您的内容：

blocks.html_en: POSSE (Publish on your Own Site, Syndicate Elsewhere): publish on your site first and then push copies to the networks, each one with a link to the original. It is the recommended technique. Your friends keep reading you where they already are, you keep the canonical copy, and if the network shuts down or bans you tomorrow, you lose nothing. The term was coined by Tantek Çelik in 2012, and it is practiced by everyone from Cory Doctorow on Pluralistic to Molly White, who repopularized it in 2024. A nice detail from the wiki: linking to the original from the copy is also "internet aikido" against spammers who copy posts, because they copy the link that credits you too.

sentences.en: POSSE (Publish on your Own Site, Syndicate Elsewhere): publish on your site first and then push copies to the networks, each one with a link to the original.
sentences.zh: POSSE（在您自己的网站上发布，在其他地方联合发布）：首先在您的网站上发布，然后将副本推送到网络，每个副本都带有指向原始版本的链接。

sentences.en: It is the recommended technique.
sentences.zh: 这是推荐的技术。

sentences.en: Your friends keep reading you where they already are, you keep the canonical copy, and if the network shuts down or bans you tomorrow, you lose nothing.
sentences.zh: 你的朋友会继续在他们已经在的地方阅读你的内容，你保留规范的副本，如果明天网络关闭或禁止你，你不会失去任何东西。

sentences.en: The term was coined by Tantek Çelik in 2012, and it is practiced by everyone from Cory Doctorow on Pluralistic to Molly White, who repopularized it in 2024.
sentences.zh: 该术语由 Tantek Çelik 于 2012 年创造，从 Pluralistic 的 Cory Doctorow 到 Molly White 等所有人都在实践，Molly White 于 2024 年重新普及了该术语。

sentences.en: A nice detail from the wiki: linking to the original from the copy is also "internet aikido" against spammers who copy posts, because they copy the link that credits you too.
sentences.zh: 维基百科上有一个很好的细节：从副本链接到原始内容也是针对复制帖子的垃圾邮件发送者的“互联网合气道”，因为他们也复制了属于您的链接。

blocks.html_en: PESOS (Publish Elsewhere, Syndicate to your Own Site): the reverse path, publishing on the silo and archiving a copy on your site afterwards. The wiki is honest about its advantages (silo apps are very polished, and it is asynchronous: if your website goes down, you can still publish), but considers it inferior: you publish subject to the silo's terms from the very first second, your copy is not the canonical one and you inherit its limits, like character caps or links wrapped in t.co.

sentences.en: PESOS (Publish Elsewhere, Syndicate to your Own Site): the reverse path, publishing on the silo and archiving a copy on your site afterwards.
sentences.zh: PESOS（在其他地方发布，联合到您自己的网站）：相反的路径，在筒仓上发布，然后在您的网站上存档副本。

sentences.en: The wiki is honest about its advantages (silo apps are very polished, and it is asynchronous: if your website goes down, you can still publish), but considers it inferior: you publish subject to the silo's terms from the very first second, your copy is not the canonical one and you inherit its limits, like character caps or links wrapped in t.co.
sentences.zh: wiki 诚实地讲述了它的优点（筒仓应用程序非常精致，而且是异步的：如果您的网站出现故障，您仍然可以发布），但认为它较差：您从第一秒开始就遵守筒仓条款，您的副本不是规范的副本，您继承了它的限制，例如字符上限或包含在 t.co 中的链接。

blocks.html_en: Backfeed: the reverse syndication of interactions. The likes, replies and reposts that your POSSE copies receive on the networks come back to your original post as webmentions. This way the full conversation is archived on your domain, safe from the next silo death. The reference service is Bridgy: it watches your copies on Mastodon, GitHub, Flickr, Reddit or Bluesky and sends you a webmention for each interaction.

sentences.en: Backfeed: the reverse syndication of interactions.
sentences.zh: 反馈：交互的反向联合。

sentences.en: The likes, replies and reposts that your POSSE copies receive on the networks come back to your original post as webmentions.
sentences.zh: 您的 POSSE 副本在网络上收到的点赞、回复和转发会作为网络提及返回到您的原始帖子。

sentences.en: This way the full conversation is archived on your domain, safe from the next silo death.
sentences.zh: 这样，完整的对话就会存档在您的域中，避免下一次孤岛死亡。

sentences.en: The reference service is Bridgy: it watches your copies on Mastodon, GitHub, Flickr, Reddit or Bluesky and sends you a webmention for each interaction.
sentences.zh: 参考服务是 Bridgy：它会在 Mastodon、GitHub、Flickr、Reddit 或 Bluesky 上查看您的副本，并向您发送每次互动的网络提及。

blocks.html_en: In short: you publish on your site, you syndicate to the silos with a link back, and the reactions come back home converted into webmentions.

sentences.en: In short: you publish on your site, you syndicate to the silos with a link back, and the reactions come back home converted into webmentions.
sentences.zh: 简而言之：您在网站上发布内容，通过返回链接联合到孤岛，然后反应返回首页并转换为网络提及。

blocks.html_en: Relationship with the Fediverse, RSS and friends

sentences.en: Relationship with the Fediverse, RSS and friends
sentences.zh: 与 Fediverse、RSS 和朋友的关系

blocks.html_en: It may surprise you, but the IndieWeb does not live isolated from the Fediverse. Fun fact: protocols like Webmention, Micropub and WebSub came out of the same place, the W3C Social Web Working Group. The same group also gave birth to the famous ActivityPub, the protocol of the Fediverse. They are first cousins, although they follow different philosophies.

sentences.en: It may surprise you, but the IndieWeb does not live isolated from the Fediverse.
sentences.zh: 您可能会感到惊讶，但 IndieWeb 并不是与 Fediverse 隔离的。

sentences.en: Fun fact: protocols like Webmention, Micropub and WebSub came out of the same place, the W3C Social Web Working Group.
sentences.zh: 有趣的事实：Webmention、Micropub 和 WebSub 等协议都来自同一个地方，即 W3C 社交网络工作组。

sentences.en: The same group also gave birth to the famous ActivityPub, the protocol of the Fediverse.
sentences.zh: 同一个团队还诞生了著名的 ActivityPub，即 Fediverse 的协议。

sentences.en: They are first cousins, although they follow different philosophies.
sentences.zh: 他们是表兄弟，尽管他们遵循不同的哲学。

blocks.html_en: The Fediverse federates servers: your identity is @user@instance, and if you don't run your own instance, you depend on someone else's. The wiki does not mince words: joining a Mastodon instance means going "from one silo, to a perhaps more open source-based and more open standards supporting silo, yet still dependent on another central org", with the aggravating factor of the admintax, the brutal cost of moderating and maintaining an instance. The IndieWeb federates websites: your identity is your domain and federation is just one more channel.

sentences.en: The Fediverse federates servers: your identity is @user@instance, and if you don't run your own instance, you depend on someone else's.
sentences.zh: Fediverse 联合服务器：您的身份是@user@instance，如果您不运行自己的实例，则需要依赖其他人的实例。

sentences.en: The wiki does not mince words: joining a Mastodon instance means going "from one silo, to a perhaps more open source-based and more open standards supporting silo, yet still dependent on another central org", with the aggravating factor of the admintax, the brutal cost of moderating and maintaining an instance.
sentences.zh: 维基百科毫不拐弯抹角：加入 Mastodon 实例意味着“从一个孤岛，转向可能更加基于开源和支持孤岛的开放标准，但仍然依赖于另一个中央组织”，其中包括管理税的加重因素，以及调节和维护实例的残酷成本。

sentences.en: The IndieWeb federates websites: your identity is your domain and federation is just one more channel.
sentences.zh: IndieWeb 联合网站：您的身份就是您的域，联合只是又一个渠道。

blocks.html_en: However, they are not incompatible. Bridgy Fed is the bridge that converts your h-card, your h-entry and your webmentions to ActivityPub (and Bluesky's AT Protocol) and vice versa. The result is that your domain becomes a Fediverse account of the form @example.com@example.com where people can find and follow you from Mastodon, and replies come back to your post as backfeed. Mastodon understands that your profile and your posts live on your site.

sentences.en: However, they are not incompatible.
sentences.zh: 然而，它们并非不兼容。

sentences.en: Bridgy Fed is the bridge that converts your h-card, your h-entry and your webmentions to ActivityPub (and Bluesky's AT Protocol) and vice versa.
sentences.zh: Bridgy Fed 是将您的 h-card、h-entry 和 webmentions 转换为 ActivityPub（以及 Bluesky 的 AT 协议）的桥梁，反之亦然。

sentences.en: The result is that your domain becomes a Fediverse account of the form @example.com@example.com where people can find and follow you from Mastodon, and replies come back to your post as backfeed.
sentences.zh: 结果是，您的域名成为 @example.com@example.com 形式的 Fediverse 帐户，人们可以在 Mastodon 中找到并关注您，并且回复会作为反馈返回到您的帖子。

sentences.en: Mastodon understands that your profile and your posts live on your site.
sentences.zh: Mastodon 知道您的个人资料和帖子都存在于您的网站上。

blocks.html_en: RSS/Atom feeds, however, are a different story, since the IndieWeb considers them formats with structural problems. The argument is the DRY principle: the content is already in your HTML, so maintaining a parallel XML copy is a "maintenance tax", a separate code path that can drift out of sync, weighs more (there are Atom files up to 4.5 times larger than their HTML equivalent) and offers a terrible experience when a human clicks on the feed link. Their alternative is h-feed: the HTML itself as the feed. But as so often happens, being technically superior does not mean being accepted. They themselves admit that very few readers support microformats. Therefore, they recommend h-feed for the IndieWeb and RSS/Atom for the rest of the planet.

sentences.en: RSS/Atom feeds, however, are a different story, since the IndieWeb considers them formats with structural problems.
sentences.zh: 然而，RSS/Atom 提要则是另一回事，因为 IndieWeb 认为它们的格式存在结构问题。

sentences.en: The argument is the DRY principle: the content is already in your HTML, so maintaining a parallel XML copy is a "maintenance tax", a separate code path that can drift out of sync, weighs more (there are Atom files up to 4.5 times larger than their HTML equivalent) and offers a terrible experience when a human clicks on the feed link.
sentences.zh: 争论的焦点是 DRY 原则：内容已经在 HTML 中，因此维护并行的 XML 副本是一项“维护税”，这是一个可能不同步的单独代码路径，重量更大（Atom 文件比 HTML 等效文件大 4.5 倍），并且当人们点击 feed 链接时会带来糟糕的体验。

sentences.en: Their alternative is h-feed: the HTML itself as the feed.
sentences.zh: 他们的替代方案是 h-feed：HTML 本身作为 feed。

sentences.en: But as so often happens, being technically superior does not mean being accepted.
sentences.zh: 但正如经常发生的那样，技术上的优越并不意味着被接受。

sentences.en: They themselves admit that very few readers support microformats.
sentences.zh: 他们自己也承认很少有读者支持微格式。

sentences.en: Therefore, they recommend h-feed for the IndieWeb and RSS/Atom for the rest of the planet.
sentences.zh: 因此，他们为 IndieWeb 推荐 h-feed，为地球上的其他地方推荐 RSS/Atom。

blocks.html_en: How to get started

sentences.en: How to get started
sentences.zh: 如何开始

blocks.html_en: The Getting Started guide recommends these steps, in this order:

sentences.en: The Getting Started guide recommends these steps, in this order:
sentences.zh: 入门指南建议按以下顺序执行这些步骤：

blocks.html_en: • Get your domain and use it as your primary identity online. A concrete tip they give: consider the registrar's whois privacy options, but only if you fully trust the provider, because ownership disputes get complicated if you are not listed as the legal owner.

sentences.en: Get your domain and use it as your primary identity online.
sentences.zh: 获取您的域名并将其用作您的主要在线身份。

sentences.en: A concrete tip they give: consider the registrar's whois privacy options, but only if you fully trust the provider, because ownership disputes get complicated if you are not listed as the legal owner.
sentences.zh: 他们给出的具体提示是：考虑注册商的 whois 隐私选项，但前提是您完全信任提供商，因为如果您没有被列为合法所有者，所有权纠纷就会变得复杂。

blocks.html_en: • Set up hosting: a managed service if you are starting out (they mention GitHub Pages, Netlify, Neocities...), self-hosting if you know what you are doing.

sentences.en: Set up hosting: a managed service if you are starting out (they mention GitHub Pages, Netlify, Neocities...), self-hosting if you know what you are doing.
sentences.zh: 设置托管：如果您刚开始，则设置托管服务（他们提到 GitHub Pages、Netlify、Neocities...）；如果您知道自己在做什么，则设置自托管。

blocks.html_en: • Create your pages: static site generator, handwritten HTML or a CMS, it doesn't matter. There is no official technology, and that is deliberate (the plurality principle).

sentences.en: Create your pages: static site generator, handwritten HTML or a CMS, it doesn't matter.
sentences.zh: 创建您的页面：静态站点生成器、手写 HTML 或 CMS，这都没关系。

sentences.en: There is no official technology, and that is deliberate (the plurality principle).
sentences.zh: 没有官方技术，这是故意的（多元化原则）。

blocks.html_en: • Syndicate to other platforms (POSSE) with links back to the original.

sentences.en: Syndicate to other platforms (POSSE) with links back to the original.
sentences.zh: 联合到其他平台 (POSSE)，并提供返回原始链接的链接。

blocks.html_en: • Add microformats: rel="me" links to your profiles on the homepage and h-entry markup on your posts.

sentences.en: Add microformats: rel="me" links to your profiles on the homepage and h-entry markup on your posts.
sentences.zh: 添加微格式：在主页上添加指向您个人资料的 rel="me" 链接，在您的帖子上添加 h-entry 标记。

blocks.html_en: • Validate your work with IndieWebify.me, which checks your rel-me, your h-card and your h-entry step by step.

sentences.en: Validate your work with IndieWebify.me, which checks your rel-me, your h-card and your h-entry step by step.
sentences.zh: 使用 IndieWebify.me 验证您的工作，它会逐步检查您的 rel-me、您的 h-card 和您的 h-entry。

blocks.html_en: • Join the community: share what you build even if it is a single page, and document it on the wiki for the next person.

sentences.en: Join the community: share what you build even if it is a single page, and document it on the wiki for the next person.
sentences.zh: 加入社区：分享您构建的内容，即使它只是一个页面，并在 wiki 上为下一个人记录它。

blocks.html_en: For those who want a more gradual path there is IndieMark, a scale of levels, an orientation guide for developers.

sentences.en: For those who want a more gradual path there is IndieMark, a scale of levels, an orientation guide for developers.
sentences.zh: 对于那些想要更渐进的道路的人来说，IndieMark 是一个等级尺度，是开发人员的方向指南。

blocks.html_en: What I have implemented (and what I have not)

sentences.en: What I have implemented (and what I have not)
sentences.zh: 我已实施的内容（以及未实施的内容）

blocks.html_en: I promised you at the beginning that I would tell you which pieces I implemented, which ones I discarded and why. Here is the list:

sentences.en: I promised you at the beginning that I would tell you which pieces I implemented, which ones I discarded and why.
sentences.zh: 我一开始就向你保证，我会告诉你我实施了哪些部分，丢弃了哪些部分以及原因。

sentences.en: Here is the list:
sentences.zh: 这是列表：

blocks.html_en: • ✅ Webmention, sending and receiving. Sending is automated with a daily task that notifies the external links of new articles, with a 24-hour margin to fix typos before telling anyone. I use the receiving side to include a list of references at the end of each article. However, I keep it separate from my comments, since those are managed by email.

sentences.en: ✅ Webmention, sending and receiving.
sentences.zh: ✅ 网络提及、发送和接收。

sentences.en: Sending is automated with a daily task that notifies the external links of new articles, with a 24-hour margin to fix typos before telling anyone.
sentences.zh: 发送是自动化的，每日任务会通知新文章的外部链接，并在告诉任何人之前有 24 小时的时间来修正拼写错误。

sentences.en: I use the receiving side to include a list of references at the end of each article.
sentences.zh: 我使用接收方在每篇文章的末尾包含参考文献列表。

sentences.en: However, I keep it separate from my comments, since those are managed by email.
sentences.zh: 不过，我将其与我的评论分开，因为这些评论是通过电子邮件管理的。

blocks.html_en: • ✅ h-entry on every article and h-card on the homepage, verified with mf2py.

sentences.en: ✅ h-entry on every article and h-card on the homepage, verified with mf2py.
sentences.zh: ✅ 主页上每篇文章和 h 卡上的 h 条目，均通过 mf2py 验证。

blocks.html_en: • ✅ rel="me" in the footer pointing to Mastodon, GitHub and Org Social, with its corresponding green checkmark.

sentences.en: ✅ rel="me" in the footer pointing to Mastodon, GitHub and Org Social, with its corresponding green checkmark.
sentences.zh: ✅ 页脚中的 rel="me" 指向 Mastodon、GitHub 和 Org Social，并带有相应的绿色复选标记。

blocks.html_en: • ❌ Micropub and IndieAuth: a conscious decision. My publishing interface is my editor and git; articles are versioned markdown. A publishing endpoint gives me nothing.

sentences.en: ❌ Micropub and IndieAuth: a conscious decision.
sentences.zh: ❌ Micropub 和 IndieAuth：一个有意识的决定。

sentences.en: My publishing interface is my editor and git; articles are versioned markdown.
sentences.zh: 我的发布界面是我的编辑器和git；文章采用 Markdown 版本。

sentences.en: A publishing endpoint gives me nothing.
sentences.zh: 发布端点没有给我任何东西。

blocks.html_en: • ❌ WebSub: evaluated and discarded. Since my publishing is deliberately delayed by 24 hours, "real time" does not justify the complexity.

sentences.en: ❌ WebSub: evaluated and discarded.
sentences.zh: ❌ WebSub：评估并丢弃。

sentences.en: Since my publishing is deliberately delayed by 24 hours, "real time" does not justify the complexity.
sentences.zh: 由于我的发布故意延迟了 24 小时，“实时”并不能证明其复杂性。

blocks.html_en: • ❌ h-feed: my article cards are reused in other contexts, such as the suggestions that appear inside each article, and marking them up would generate ambiguous h-entries for parsers. If I had designed the templates with this structure in mind, it would have been different. RSS covers that role just fine.

sentences.en: ❌ h-feed: my article cards are reused in other contexts, such as the suggestions that appear inside each article, and marking them up would generate ambiguous h-entries for parsers.
sentences.zh: ❌ h-feed：我的文章卡在其他上下文中重复使用，例如每篇文章中出现的建议，对它们进行标记会为解析器生成不明确的 h-条目。

sentences.en: If I had designed the templates with this structure in mind, it would have been different.
sentences.zh: 如果我在设计模板时考虑到这种结构，情况就会有所不同。

sentences.en: RSS covers that role just fine.
sentences.zh: RSS 很好地发挥了这一作用。

blocks.html_en: But the advice that helped me the most is not technical, it is about philosophy and web design ethics. Scattered across the wiki, it is worth gold with or without the IndieWeb. My selection:

sentences.en: But the advice that helped me the most is not technical, it is about philosophy and web design ethics.
sentences.zh: 但对我帮助最大的建议不是技术性的，而是关于哲学和网页设计道德的。

sentences.en: Scattered across the wiki, it is worth gold with or without the IndieWeb.
sentences.zh: 分散在整个 wiki 中，无论有没有 IndieWeb，它都价值不菲。

sentences.en: My selection:
sentences.zh: 我的选择：

blocks.html_en: • Plain HTML is the most durable format. You can read the whole web without using JavaScript.

sentences.en: Plain HTML is the most durable format.
sentences.zh: 纯 HTML 是最持久的格式。

sentences.en: You can read the whole web without using JavaScript.
sentences.zh: 您无需使用 JavaScript 即可阅读整个网络。

blocks.html_en: • Cool URIs don't change: design permalinks you can maintain forever. For example, I can change the slug of an article and it will still work.

sentences.en: Cool URIs don't change: design permalinks you can maintain forever.
sentences.zh: 酷 URI 不会改变：设计可以永久维护的永久链接。

sentences.en: For example, I can change the slug of an article and it will still work.
sentences.zh: 例如，我可以更改文章的标题，它仍然有效。

blocks.html_en: • Gradual commitment, silo by silo: no exodus needed. Every type of content you start publishing on your own site first is a step forward in owning your data.

sentences.en: Gradual commitment, silo by silo: no exodus needed.
sentences.zh: 逐步承诺，逐个筒仓：无需外流。

sentences.en: Every type of content you start publishing on your own site first is a step forward in owning your data.

sentences.en: Think about extreme longevity: the wiki seriously discusses what happens to your website when you die, from the "dead man's switch" that hands the keys to someone you trust, to the practical problem Peter Molnar sums up as: "who's going to pay for your domain if you're not?".
sentences.zh: 想想极端的长寿：维基百科严肃地讨论了当你死后你的网站会发生什么，从将钥匙交给你信任的人的“死人开关”，到彼得·莫尔纳总结的实际问题：“如果你不死，谁会为你的域名付费？”。

content_html_zh:
在好奇心的驱使下，我决定找出一些博客和 Mastodon toots 上不断出现的 IndieWeb 标签到底是什么意思。 我很快发现这不仅仅是一个空洞的概念，也不仅仅是对 90 年代网络的怀旧之情。 这是一场具有具体想法、协议和活跃社区的运动！ 当我阅读他们的维基时，我越来越了解他们的立场和他们提案的智慧。 由于我的热情，我决定听从他们的建议并采用对我的网站有意义的协议。 完成测试和调整后，我可以确认体验非常积极：我学到了很多东西，我网站的用户体验得到了更多改善，而且我很享受这个过程。

这就是为什么我决定写这篇文章，清理过去几个月的笔记。 也许它会帮助其他人发现它，并一路改善网络的健康状况。 对于那些爱管闲事的人，我还会告诉你我实施了哪些部分，丢弃了哪些部分以及原因，以及哪些建议被证明是最有用的。

但首先，我们需要回答一个基本问题。

IndieWeb 将自己定义为“以人为本的企业网络替代方案”。 它提出的不是一个软件或一个框架，而是一个思想基础。 它有意包含多种方法和项目。 正如其主页所说：“我们以人为本，而不是以项目为中心”。

这一切都始于 2010 年，当时 Aaron Parecki 和 Tantek Çelik 参加了在波特兰举行的联邦社交网络峰会。 他们离开时感觉需要一种不同的方法：更少的协议和更多的创建者。 2011 年，第一届 IndieWebCamp 在波特兰举行，此后每年都会在世界各地庆祝，此外还有 Homebrew 网站俱乐部，人们聚在一起改进他们的个人网站。

定义它的三大支柱是：

  • 您的内容是您的：当您在网络上发布某些内容时，它应该属于您，而不是公司。 太多的公司已经关闭并带走了用户的数据。
  • 您可以更好地连接：您的文章可以分发到任何平台，而不仅仅是一个平台，来自其他服务的回复和点赞可以返回到您的网站，因此您可以将所有内容集中在一处。
  • 您尽在掌控：您可以以任何您想要的格式发布任何您想要的内容，并且具有始终有效的可读且永久的 URL。

因此，我们可以说它是一个由共享这些承诺的个人独立网站组成的社区。

这也是为什么它不禁止你使用社交网络，但它确实与围墙花园作斗争。

敌人有一个名字：筒仓

整个 IndieWeb 词汇都是与一个概念相对立的：筒仓，也称为围墙花园。 维基将其定义为一个集中式网站，通常由营利性公司拥有，该网站对您贡献的内容拥有某些权利，并以某种方式限制访问。 其特点：

  • 他们要求您创建一个特定于站点的帐户才能参与。
  • 它们只允许您与同一网站上的其他帐户进行交互。
  • 他们通常会添加：限制性服务条款、对您在内部创建的内容的许可声明、阻止索引的墙壁或导入和导出内容的障碍。

为什么这是一个问题？ 因为筒仓会消亡，当它们消亡时，您的内容也会随之消失。 网站死亡页面（“不可思议的旅程结束的地方”，这是对我们不可思议的旅程的企业委婉说法）保留了毁灭性的年表：

  • GeoCities：雅虎于 2009 年 10 月 26 日将其关闭。 2300 万页消失了。
  • MySpace：2019 年，在服务器迁移过程中，它丢失了前 12 年上传的所有音乐，来自 1400 万艺术家的超过 5000 万首歌曲。
  • Google+：于 2019 年 4 月关闭。
  • Posterous、FriendFeed、Vine、雅虎群组、TinyLetter、Cohost... 该列表不断增长，甚至有“即将死亡”和收购的部分，这往往预示着它们。

筒仓甚至不需要消亡：网络默认情况下是脆弱的。 根据皮尤研究中心 2024 年的一项研究，2013 年存在的网页中有 38% 在 10 年后不再可访问。

IndieWeb 的结论并不是“不要使用社交网络”。 它更微妙：使用您想要的任何内容，但要确保内容的规范副本位于您控制的域中。

这就是为什么它定义了一套原则来对抗网络的脆弱性。

原则

该社区遵循 11 条原则：

  1. 拥有您的数据：您的内容、元数据和身份都位于您的域下，并且随着时间的推移，您保留对它们的访问权限。
  2. 使用和发布可见数据：首先是人类，其次是机器。 如果 HTML 可以承载数据，则无需并行 API。
  3. 制作您需要的东西：为自己构建工具，而不是为某个假设的用户构建工具。 “如果你为某个假设的用户设计，他们可能实际上并不存在；如果你为自己设计，那么你确实存在。”
  4. 使用你所做的：使用你每天构建的东西。 “如果你都不依赖它，为什么其他人要依赖它呢？”
  5. 记录你的东西：你已经有了一个表达你的想法的地方，用它来记录你的流程、想法和代码。 你帮助别人和未来的自己。
  6. 开源你的东西：不是强制性的，但它可以帮助其他人更快地进入独立网络。
  7. UX 先于协议：用户体验第一，然后是足以支持它的最简单、最小的协议，仅此而已。 他们将其总结为“先于管道的用户体验”。
  8. 模块化：小的、松散耦合的部分，因此您不依赖于特定的设备、语言或平台。
  9. 长寿：为长网络而构建。 “如果人类社会能够保存古代纸莎草纸、维多利亚时代的照片和恐龙骨骼，我们就应该能够建立网络技术，而不需要我们每隔几年以进步的名义摧毁我们所做的一切。”
  10. 多元化：刻意鼓励多元化的方法使社区比任何单一文化都更具弹性。
  11. 最重要的是，玩得开心：记住 90 年代的网络、GeoCities、响亮的背景和动画 GIF。 “它可能很丑陋并且编码很糟糕，但很有趣。 保持网络怪异和有趣。”

编号仅供参考，并非优先级。 社区并不要求你满足所有这些要求，但它确实要求你牢记它们。

但 IndieWeb 并不仅仅依靠原则生存。 有一组标准可以帮助您的网站更加开放、具有互操作性并防止消失。

技术部分

IndieWeb 并没有发明一个平台，而是创造了一个平台。它定义了一些相互组合的小标准。 官方索引按年龄和实施广度对它们进行排序。

我们一一来说吧。

起点：您的域

它不是一个协议，但它是其他一切的先决条件：您自己的域用作您的主要在线身份。 这是入门指南的第一步，也是社区认为“在”IndieWeb 上所需的最低限度。 如果明天您在保留域名的情况下更改托管或 CMS，您的所有链接、读者和搜索排名都会在迁移后继续存在。

微格式2：您的 HTML 就是您的 API

microformats2 解决了一个问题：使您的内容可供机器读取，而无需发布并行文件或构建 API。

该实现非常优雅，因为它使用您添加到已有 HTML 中的 CSS 类。 前缀表示数据类型：h-* 表示根，p-* 表示纯文本，u-* 表示 URL，dt-* 表示日期，e-* 表示嵌入 HTML。 两个基本词汇：

h-card 是您的身份：相当于在线名片。 只需在主页上显示最少的姓名、URL 和照片，读者就会在您的帖子旁边显示您的个人资料，并且应用程序会识别您。 它的工作原理类似于基于域名的 Gravatar，而不是基于电子邮件的 Gravatar：

<a class="h-card" href="https://example.com">
  <img src="/photo.png" alt="" />
  Jane Doe
</a>

h-entry 是内容的单位：帖子的标记。 维基百科称其为“独立网络的关键构建模块”：

<article class="h-entry">
  <h1 class="p-name">Article title</h1>
  <p>By <a class="p-author h-card" href="https://example.com">Jane Doe</a>,
     <time class="dt-published" datetime="2026-07-19">July 19, 2026</time></p>
  <div class="e-content">
    <p>The post content...</p>
  </div>
</article>

还有 h-feed，它将几个 h-entry 元素组合在一起，将您的列表页面变成可以直接从 HTML 订阅的 feed。

维基用我喜欢的一句话总结了这一点：

您的网站就是您的 API

用于阅读、微格式；对于写作，Micropub（我们会到达那里）。

rel-me 是最简单的作品，也是效果最直接的作品。 链接上的一个属性，表示“此链接的目标代表与当前页面相同的人”：

<a href="https://mastodon.social/@jane" rel="me">Mastodon</a>

验证需要互惠：您的网站链接到个人资料，个人资料链接回您的网站，两者都带有 rel="me"。 这样，您就可以获得分布式身份验证，无需涉及中央机构。 这正是 Mastodon 绿色验证复选标记背后的机制：如果您的页面使用 rel-me 链接到您的个人资料，并且您的个人资料链接回来，Mastodon 会显示您的域名已验证。 它还受到 Threads、PixelFed、GitHub、Keybase 和 Wikipedia 的支持。

RelMeAuth 位于 rel-me 之上：使用您的个人 URL 对服务进行身份验证，将身份证明委托给您主页链接到的 OAuth 提供商（如 GitHub）。 它是 IndieLogin 等服务的基础。

Webmention：站点之间的对话

Webmention 是明星标准，自 2017 年 1 月 12 日起成为 W3C 推荐标准，也是 Pingback 的现代继承者。 它解决了网站之间的对话：从网络到网络的评论、点赞、回复和转发，中间没有平台。 许多人用它作为 Disqus 的替代品。

流程故意简单：

  1. 我写了一篇文章，链接到您的一篇文章。
  2. 我的服务器访问您的文章并查找您的端点：HTTP 标头链接：<...>; HTML 中的 rel="webmention" 或 <link rel="webmention">。
  3. 它发送一个仅包含两个参数的 POST：源（我的帖子）和目标（您的）。
POST /webmention HTTP/1.1
Host: your-site.com
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

source=https://my-site.com/my-post&target=https://your-site.com/your-article
  1. 您的服务器验证提及：规范要求下载源并检查它是否确实包含指向目标的链接。 如果没有这种验证，任何人都可以伪造虚假提及。
  2. 验证后，您的网站将决定如何处理它。 这就是微格式的用武之地：通过解析源的 h-entry，您可以知道它是回复 (u-in-reply-to)、点赞 (u-like-of) 还是转发 (u-repost-of)，而作者的 h-card 可以让您像在任何评论部分一样显示他们的姓名和照片。

如果您觉得这听起来像一个社交网络，那是因为它确实如此！ 每个站点都是网络中的一个节点，它们之间的链接形成社交图谱。

它不是一个完美的系统，因为它与任何其他去中心化网络都存在同样的问题，这就是为什么有一些扩展可以攻击它的弱点：

  • 凭证，防止垃圾邮件：网络提及带有第三个参数，其中包含“凭证”的 URL，该网站是您已知的链接到发件人域的网站。 它将过滤成本从接收者转移到发送者。
  • Salmention，传播主题：如果有人回复我帖子的评论，原始帖子会通过向所有相关人员重新发送更新网络提及来发现。

您无法逃脱垃圾邮件或审核。 然而，它是构建分布式评论系统的良好基础。

如果您的网站没有后端怎么办？ 您不会被排除在外：webmention.io 代表您接收网络提及。 您在 HTML 中添加一个指向该服务的 <link>，它为您提供了一个 API 来查询和显示它们。 如果您使用 Hugo、Jekyll 或 Eleventy 等静态站点生成器，这正是您所需要的。

IndieAuth：您的域名作为您的登录名

IndieAuth 回答了这个问题：如果您的登录身份是您的 URL，而不是“您在 Google 上”或“您在 Facebook 上”，该怎么办？ 从技术上讲，它是 OAuth 2.0，身份验证和授权的标准。 用户和应用程序都通过 URL 进行标识，这消除了事先客户端注册的需要（“IndieAuth 使用 DNS 作为客户端注册的替代品”），并且 PKCE 是强制性的（一种防止攻击者窃取访问令牌的安全机制）。

简而言之，流程是：您在登录表单中输入您的域名，服务获取您的页面并通过 rel="indieauth-metadata" 发现您的授权服务器，将您重定向到那里，您可以根据自己的喜好进行身份验证（密码、电子邮件、RelMeAuth），并且服务会收到您控制该 URL 的确认信息。 这是社会认为稳定的生活水平。

Micropub：从任何客户端发布

Micropub 自 2017 年 5 月起成为 W3C 推荐标准，它将发布界面与您网站的软件分开：任何应用程序（Web、iOS、Android）都可以在您的域上创建、编辑和删除帖子。 它使用通过 IndieAuth 获得的 OAuth 令牌取代了旧的 MetaWeblog 和 AtomPub（它们依赖于共享您的密码）。

它的美妙之处在于它的词汇：它不是发明词汇，而是序列化的微格式。 创建帖子是一个带有 h=entry 和相同 h-entry 属性的 POST：

curl https://your-site.com/micropub \
  -d h=entry \
  -d "content=Hello world" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer XXXXXXX"

如果您已经习惯使用 REST API，那么这会感觉非常自然。

WebSub：实时提要

WebSub（以前称为 PubSubHubbub）是自 2018 年 1 月起成为 W3C 推荐标准，它删除了 Feed 轮询：不再有一千名读者每半小时询问您的服务器是否有新内容，而是您在发布时通知中心，中心会立即通过 Webhooks 通知所有订阅者。 它减少了服务器的负载并且更新立即到达。

许多提要阅读器和聚合器都支持 WebSub，例如 Feedly 或 NewsBlur。

Microsub：解耦阅读器

Microsub 是最年轻的标准，目前仍处于草案阶段。 将其视为将所有内容连接到社交阅读应用程序中的基础设施。

有 2 层：执行管道操作（管理订阅、获取和解析提要、规范化数据）的服务器和仅呈现阅读界面的客户端。 这样，客户就可以在用户体验上进行竞争，并且您的订阅可以在他们之间移植。 结合Micropub的读者回复和Webmention的通知，构成了IndieWeb“社交阅读器”的完整架构。

发布策略：POSSE、PESOS 和反馈

除了原则和协议之外，IndieWeb 还提供了一种与社交网络共存的心理模型，而无需向其提供您的内容：

POSSE（在您自己的网站上发布，在其他地方联合发布）：首先在您的网站上发布，然后将副本推送到网络，每个副本都带有指向原始版本的链接。 这是推荐的技术。 你的朋友会继续在他们已经在的地方阅读你的内容，你保留规范的副本，如果明天网络关闭或禁止你，你不会失去任何东西。 该术语由 Tantek Çelik 于 2012 年创造，从 Pluralistic 的 Cory Doctorow 到 Molly White 等所有人都在实践，Molly White 于 2024 年重新普及了该术语。 维基百科上有一个很好的细节：从副本链接到原始内容也是针对复制帖子的垃圾邮件发送者的“互联网合气道”，因为他们也复制了属于您的链接。

PESOS（在其他地方发布，联合到您自己的网站）：相反的路径，在筒仓上发布，然后在您的网站上存档副本。 wiki 诚实地讲述了它的优点（筒仓应用程序非常精致，而且是异步的：如果您的网站出现故障，您仍然可以发布），但认为它较差：您从第一秒开始就遵守筒仓条款，您的副本不是规范的副本，您继承了它的限制，例如字符上限或包含在 t.co 中的链接。

反馈：交互的反向联合。 您的 POSSE 副本在网络上收到的点赞、回复和转发会作为网络提及返回到您的原始帖子。 这样，完整的对话就会存档在您的域中，避免下一次孤岛死亡。 参考服务是 Bridgy：它会在 Mastodon、GitHub、Flickr、Reddit 或 Bluesky 上查看您的副本，并向您发送每次互动的网络提及。

简而言之：您在网站上发布内容，通过返回链接联合到孤岛，然后反应返回首页并转换为网络提及。

与 Fediverse、RSS 和朋友的关系

您可能会感到惊讶，但 IndieWeb 并不是与 Fediverse 隔离的。 有趣的事实：Webmention、Micropub 和 WebSub 等协议都来自同一个地方，即 W3C 社交网络工作组。 同一个团队还诞生了著名的 ActivityPub，即 Fediverse 的协议。 他们是表兄弟，尽管他们遵循不同的哲学。

Fediverse 联合服务器：您的身份是@user@instance，如果您不运行自己的实例，则需要依赖其他人的实例。 维基百科毫不拐弯抹角：加入 Mastodon 实例意味着“从一个孤岛，转向可能更加基于开源和支持孤岛的开放标准，但仍然依赖于另一个中央组织”，其中包括管理税的加重因素，以及调节和维护实例的残酷成本。 IndieWeb 联合网站：您的身份就是您的域，联合只是又一个渠道。

然而，它们并非不兼容。 Bridgy Fed 是将您的 h-card、h-entry 和 webmentions 转换为 ActivityPub（以及 Bluesky 的 AT 协议）的桥梁，反之亦然。 结果是，您的域名成为 @example.com@example.com 形式的 Fediverse 帐户，人们可以在 Mastodon 中找到并关注您，并且回复会作为反馈返回到您的帖子。 Mastodon 知道您的个人资料和帖子都存在于您的网站上。

然而，RSS/Atom 提要则是另一回事，因为 IndieWeb 认为它们的格式存在结构问题。 争论的焦点是 DRY 原则：内容已经在 HTML 中，因此维护并行的 XML 副本是一项“维护税”，这是一个可能不同步的单独代码路径，重量更大（Atom 文件比 HTML 等效文件大 4.5 倍），并且当人们点击 feed 链接时会带来糟糕的体验。 他们的替代方案是 h-feed：HTML 本身作为 feed。 但正如经常发生的那样，技术上的优越并不意味着被接受。 他们自己也承认很少有读者支持微格式。 因此，他们为 IndieWeb 推荐 h-feed，为地球上的其他地方推荐 RSS/Atom。

如何开始

入门指南建议按以下顺序执行这些步骤：

  1. 获取您的域名并将其用作您的主要在线身份。 他们给出的具体提示是：考虑注册商的 whois 隐私选项，但前提是您完全信任提供商，因为如果您没有被列为合法所有者，所有权纠纷就会变得复杂。
  2. 设置托管：如果您刚开始，则设置托管服务（他们提到 GitHub Pages、Netlify、Neocities...）；如果您知道自己在做什么，则设置自托管。
  3. 创建您的页面：静态站点生成器、手写 HTML 或 CMS，这都没关系。 没有官方技术，这是故意的（多元化原则）。
  4. 联合到其他平台 (POSSE)，并提供返回原始链接的链接。
  5. 添加微格式：在主页上添加指向您个人资料的 rel="me" 链接，在您的帖子上添加 h-entry 标记。
  6. 使用 IndieWebify.me 验证您的工作，它会逐步检查您的 rel-me、您的 h-card 和您的 h-entry。
  7. 加入社区：分享您构建的内容，即使它只是一个页面，并在 wiki 上为下一个人记录它。

对于那些想要更渐进的道路的人来说，IndieMark 是一个等级尺度，是开发人员的方向指南。

我已实施的内容（以及未实施的内容）

我一开始就向你保证，我会告诉你我实施了哪些部分，丢弃了哪些部分以及原因。 这是列表：

  • ✅ 网络提及、发送和接收。 发送是自动化的，每日任务会通知新文章的外部链接，并在告诉任何人之前有 24 小时的时间来修正拼写错误。 我使用接收方在每篇文章的末尾包含参考文献列表。 不过，我将其与我的评论分开，因为这些评论是通过电子邮件管理的。
  • ✅ 主页上每篇文章和 h 卡上的 h 条目，均通过 mf2py 验证。
  • ✅ 页脚中的 rel="me" 指向 Mastodon、GitHub 和 Org Social，并带有相应的绿色复选标记。
  • ❌ Micropub 和 IndieAuth：一个有意识的决定。 我的发布界面是我的编辑器和git；文章采用 Markdown 版本。 发布端点没有给我任何东西。
  • ❌ WebSub：评估并丢弃。 由于我的发布故意延迟了 24 小时，“实时”并不能证明其复杂性。
  • ❌ h-feed：我的文章卡在其他上下文中重复使用，例如每篇文章中出现的建议，对它们进行标记会为解析器生成不明确的 h-条目。 如果我在设计模板时考虑到这种结构，情况就会有所不同。 RSS 很好地发挥了这一作用。

但对我帮助最大的建议不是技术性的，而是关于哲学和网页设计道德的。 分散在整个 wiki 中，无论有没有 IndieWeb，它都价值不菲。 我的选择：

  • 纯 HTML 是最持久的格式。 您无需使用 JavaScript 即可阅读整个网络。
  • 酷 URI 不会改变：设计可以永久维护的永久链接。 例如，我可以更改文章的标题，它仍然有效。
  • 逐步承诺，逐个筒仓：无需外流。 您首先在自己的网站上开始发布的每种类型的内容都是在拥有数据方面向前迈出的一步。
  • 想想极端的长寿：维基百科严肃地讨论了当你死后你的网站会发生什么，从将钥匙交给你信任的人的“死人开关”，到彼得·莫尔纳总结的实际问题：“如果你不死，谁会为你的域名付费？”。

和... 玩得开心。 一个不完美、奇怪的个人网站比你厌倦维护的一尘不染的模板更有价值。
